generation_method: AI-assisted (Claude Opus) with human review pending

ksas.statement: Knowledge of change management frameworks (ADKAR, Kotter, McKinsey 7S) and how they are applied to data and AI transformation initiatives with multi-year timelines.
Knowledge of organizational adoption barriers specific to data and AI, including technical resistance, skill gaps, cultural inertia, legacy tool dependencies, and competing executive priorities.
Knowledge of stakeholder analysis and communication strategies, including identifying change agents, managing resistant stakeholders, and tailoring messaging to different organizational levels.
Skill in designing and executing data and AI adoption roadmaps, including capability maturity assessment, phased implementation planning, and success metric definition.
Skill in developing and delivering change communication and training materials, including use case documentation, business value articulation, and hands-on guidance for different user personas.
Ability to navigate organizational politics and resistance to change, including building coalitions, managing executive disagreement, and maintaining momentum through setbacks.
Conduct organizational readiness assessments for data and AI adoption, including capability maturity analysis, stakeholder interviews, and identification of adoption barriers.
Manage adoption communications and engagement, including producing use case documentation, delivering training programs, and monitoring adoption metrics and user satisfaction.
Knowledge of AI audit frameworks and standards, including ISACA Advanced in AI Audit (AAIA), IIA AI Auditing Framework, AICPA AI audit guidance, and ISO-42001 compliance objectives.
Knowledge of AI lifecycle audit considerations, including development governance, data provenance, model validation, deployment controls, monitoring, and incident response procedures.
Knowledge of AI risk and control objectives relevant to audit, including model risk management, algorithmic fairness assessment, explainability verification, and regulatory compliance validation.
Knowledge of AI testing methodologies for audit purposes, including adversarial testing, model performance evaluation, data quality assessment, and prompt injection vulnerability scanning.
Skill in planning AI system audits, including scoping AI applications, defining audit procedures, identifying control gaps, and documenting findings with evidence-based recommendations.
Skill in evaluating AI governance structures, including model governance committees, incident response procedures, and remediation tracking for audit findings.
Ability to communicate AI audit findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, translating technical control gaps into business risk language.
Execute AI system audits, including control assessment, testing procedures, and comprehensive documentation of findings, recommendations, and management remediation plans.
Monitor AI governance control effectiveness through periodic audit reviews, follow-up assessments, and remediation tracking.
Knowledge of quantitative trading strategies, including momentum-based strategies, mean reversion, statistical arbitrage, machine learning-based approaches, and their mathematical foundations.
Knowledge of financial instruments and market microstructure, including equities, derivatives, foreign exchange, fixed income, and the execution dynamics that affect algorithmic trading performance.
Knowledge of backtesting methodologies and limitations, including historical data biases, overfitting risks, survivorship bias, and walk-forward validation techniques.
Knowledge of regulatory and compliance requirements for algorithmic trading, including SEC Rule 10b-5, UK FCA standards, MiFID II, circuit breakers, and market abuse regulations.
Skill in designing and implementing trading algorithms using quantitative finance libraries (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn, TensorFlow) and backtesting frameworks.
Skill in optimizing trading strategies for execution efficiency, including slippage estimation, market impact modeling, and order execution algorithms (VWAP, TWAP, Implementation Shortfall).
Ability to evaluate strategy performance under adverse market conditions, including stress testing for market crises, black swan events, and liquidity shocks.
Develop and backtest algorithmic trading strategies, including strategy research, parameter optimization, risk assessment, and documentation of methodology.
Monitor live trading algorithms, including performance analysis, anomaly detection, and rapid intervention capability for market disruptions or strategy degradation.
Knowledge of EU AI Act requirements, including prohibited practices, high-risk AI system definitions, transparency obligations, and the roles and responsibilities of authorized representatives.

Knowledge of AI system documentation requirements under EU AI Act, including technical documentation, training and testing data requirements, model card specifications, and conformity assessment evidence.

Knowledge of related regulatory frameworks applicable to AI, including GDPR data protection, sector-specific regulations (MedTech, financial services), product safety standards, and environmental requirements.
Knowledge of notified body assessment procedures and competency requirements under EU AI Act, including the role of conformity assessment bodies and third-party audit processes.
Skill in assessing AI system classification and risk categorization under EU AI Act, including determining high-risk status and identifying applicable compliance obligations.
Skill in coordinating compliance activities between AI developers and European regulatory authorities, including technical documentation preparation and submission to notified bodies.
Ability to navigate multi-jurisdictional AI regulation, including EU AI Act, national implementations, and sector-specific AI regulations across different European member states.
Review AI system technical documentation for EU AI Act compliance, including verification of training data documentation, model card completeness, and risk assessment evidence.
Prepare and submit AI system conformity assessment documentation to notified bodies, ensuring completeness and alignment with EU AI Act technical requirements.
Knowledge of AI application architectures and patterns, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agentic workflows, multi-model ensembles, and fine-tuning approaches.
Knowledge of model serving platforms and deployment options, including cloud-based services (AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP Vertex AI), on-premises solutions, and edge deployment considerations.
Knowledge of AI solution business case development, including cost-benefit analysis, ROI calculation, risk assessment, and the comparison of build-vs-buy decisions for AI capabilities.
Knowledge of production AI system monitoring and governance, including model performance degradation detection, drift monitoring, feedback loops, and retraining procedures.
Skill in designing end-to-end AI solutions for specific business problems, including requirements gathering, architecture design, technology stack selection, and success metric definition.
Skill in communicating technical AI architecture and limitations to non-technical stakeholders, including feasibility assessment, timeline estimation, and risk communication.
Ability to prototype and pilot AI solutions rapidly, demonstrating proof-of-concept value before committing to full production deployment.
Develop AI solution architecture and design documentation, including component specification, data flow, deployment topology, and operational runbooks.
Build and validate AI solution prototypes, including data pipeline development, model integration, and performance benchmarking against success criteria.
